across.clue: to say that something such as a fact is not true or correct
Feeling emotionally or physically unsteady | ___ at the knees
to move or travel with no particular purpose.

Overcome or defeat | Get the ___ of

down.clue: Ultimately good, even if it seems bad | All for the ___
Hold an opinion | Take the ___ that...
someone who has a position after someone else
To think about or discuss something very carefully especially before making a decision.

to protect someone against a particular disease by injecting a medicine containing a small amount of the disease into them so that their body becomes immune to it.

to feel very happy about something or to celebrate something in a happy way.
